title: Lovely Earthy French Cheese Tagine
description: A rich and earthy French cheese tagine featuring stout and vegetarian-friendly ingredients, perfect for a cozy dinner on a rainy evening. The dish is cooked in a tagine pot, resulting in a delicious cheese-pull effect.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked mushroom mixture, grated Comte cheese, stout beer, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Transfer the mixture to a tagine pot or a large Dutch oven.
Cover the pot and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
